texto: I had seen enough of Western courtship rituals to know European families did little to identify, research and arrange suitable marriages. Instead, to catch the attention of potential partners, Western males had to resort to shows of strength, aggression and virility, rather like wild boars in the mating season, and Western females had to decorate themselves and their homes, like bower birds.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ovidia Yu: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I had seen enough of Western courtship rituals to know European families did little to identify, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, marriage, western pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e reflexao.
